Α.gloss: the first letter of the alphabet; figuratively, only (from its use as a numeral) the first

ἀγαθοποιέω.gloss: to be a well-doer (as a favor or a duty)

ἀγαλλιάω.gloss: properly, to jump for joy, i.e. exult

ἀγανακτέω.gloss: to be greatly afflicted, i.e. (figuratively) indignant

ἀγαπάω.gloss: to love (in a social or moral sense)

ἀγάπη.gloss: love, i.e. affection or benevolence; specially (plural) a love-feast

ἄγγελος.gloss: compare ) (to bring tidings); a messenger; especially an "angel"; by implication, a pastor

ἀγενής.gloss: properly, without kin, i.e. (of unknown descent, and by implication) ignoble

ἁγιάζω.gloss: to make holy, i.e. (ceremonially) purify or consecrate; (mentally) to venerate

ἁγιασμός.gloss: properly, purification, i.e. (the state) purity; concretely (by Hebraism) a purifier

ἅγιος.gloss: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)

ἁγνίζω.gloss: to make clean, i.e. (figuratively) sanctify (ceremonially or morally)

ἀγνοέω.gloss: not to know (through lack of information or intelligence); by implication, to ignore (through disinclination)

ἁγνός.gloss: properly, clean, i.e. (figuratively) innocent, modest, perfect

ἀγορά.gloss: properly, the town-square (as a place of public resort); by implication, a market or thoroughfare

ἀγοράζω.gloss: properly, to go to market, i.e. (by implication) to purchase; specially, to redeem

ἀγοραῖος.gloss: relating to the market-place, i.e. forensic (times); by implication, vulgar

ἄγρα.gloss: (abstractly) a catching (of fish); also (concretely) a haul (of fish)

ἄγριος.gloss: wild (as pertaining to the country), literally (natural) or figuratively (fierce)

Ἀγρίππας.gloss: wild-horse tamer; Agrippas, one of the Herods

ἀγρός.gloss: a field (as a drive for cattle); genitive case, the country; specially, a farm, i.e. hamlet

ἀγρυπνέω.gloss: to be sleepless, i.e. keep awake

ἄγω.gloss: properly, to lead; by implication, to bring, drive, (reflexively) go, (specially) pass (time), or (figuratively) induce

ἀγωγή.gloss: a bringing up, i.e. mode of living

ἀγών.gloss: properly, a place of assembly (as if led), i.e. (by implication) a contest (held there); figuratively, an effort or anxiety

ἀγωνία.gloss: a struggle (properly, the state), i.e. (figuratively) anguish

ἀγωνίζομαι.gloss: to struggle, literally (to compete for a prize), figuratively (to contend with an adversary), or genitive case (to endeavor to accomplish something)

ἀδελφός.gloss: a brother (literally or figuratively) near or remote (much like )

ἀδελφότης.gloss: brotherhood (properly, the feeling of brotherliness), i.e. the (Christian) fraternity

ᾅδης.gloss: properly, unseen, i.e. "Hades" or the place (state) of departed souls

ἀδιαλείπτως.gloss: uninterruptedly, i.e. without omission (on an appropriate occasion)

ἀδικέω.gloss: to be unjust, i.e. (actively) do wrong (morally, socially or physically)

ἀδικία.gloss: (legal) injustice (properly, the quality, by implication, the act); morally, wrongfulness (of character, life or act)

ἄδικος.gloss: unjust; by extension wicked; by implication, treacherous; specially, heathen

ἀδόκιμος.gloss: unapproved, i.e. rejected; by implication, worthless (literally or morally)

ἀδύνατος.gloss: unable, i.e. weak (literally or figuratively); passively, impossible

ἀεί.gloss: "ever," by qualification regularly; by implication, earnestly

ἄζυμος.gloss: unleavened, i.e. (figuratively) uncorrupted; (in the neutral plural) specially (by implication) the Passover week

ἀθετέω.gloss: to set aside, i.e. (by implication) to disesteem, neutralize or violate

Ἀθηναῖος.gloss: an Athenæan or inhabitant of Athenæ

ἀθλέω.gloss: to contend in the competitive games

αἰγιαλός.gloss: a beach (on which the waves dash)

Αἰγύπτιος.gloss: an Ægyptian or inhabitant of Ægyptus

Αἴγυπτος.gloss: Ægyptus, the land of the Nile

ἀΐδιος.gloss: everduring (forward and backward, or forward only)

αἰδώς.gloss: bashfulness, i.e. (towards men), modesty or (towards God) awe

αἷμα.gloss: blood, literally (of men or animals), figuratively (the juice of grapes) or specially (the atoning blood of Christ); by implication, bloodshed, also kindred

αἱμοῤῥέω.gloss: to flow blood, i.e. have a hæmorrhage

αἴνεσις.gloss: a praising (the act), i.e. (specially) a thank(-offering)

αἶνος.gloss: properly, a story, but used in the sense of ; praise (of God)

αἱρέομαι.gloss: to take for oneself, i.e. to prefer

αἵρεσις.gloss: properly, a choice, i.e. (specially) a party or (abstractly) disunion

αἴρω.gloss: to lift up; by implication, to take up or away; figuratively, to raise (the voice), keep in suspense (the mind), specially, to sail away (i.e. weigh anchor); by Hebraism (compare ) to expiate sin

αἰσθητήριον.gloss: properly, an organ of perception, i.e. (figuratively) judgment

αἴτημα.gloss: a thing asked or (abstractly) an asking

αἰτία.gloss: a cause (as if asked for), i.e. (logical) reason (motive, matter), (legal) crime (alleged or proved)

αἰχμάλωτος.gloss: properly, a prisoner of war, i.e. (genitive case) a captive

αἰών.gloss: properly, an age; by extension, perpetuity (also past); by implication, the world; specially (Jewish) a Messianic period (present or future)

αἰώνιος.gloss: perpetual (also used of past time, or past and future as well)

ἀκαιρέομαι.gloss: to be inopportune (for oneself), i.e. to fail of a proper occasion

ἄκακος.gloss: not bad, i.e. (objectively) innocent or (subjectively) unsuspecting

ἀκμάζω.gloss: to make a point, i.e. (figuratively) mature

ἀκοή.gloss: hearing (the act, the sense or the thing heard)

ἀκολουθέω.gloss: properly, to be in the same way with, i.e. to accompany (specially, as a disciple)

ἀκρίς.gloss: a locust (as pointed, or as lighting on the top of vegetation)

ἀκροβυστία.gloss: the prepuce; by implication, an uncircumcised (i.e. gentile, figuratively, unregenerate) state or person

ἀκροθίνιον.gloss: properly (in the plural) the top of the heap, i.e. (by implication) best of the booty

ἀκωλύτως.gloss: in an unhindered manner, i.e. freely

Ἀλεξανδρεύς.gloss: an Alexandreian or inhabitant of Alexandria

Ἀλέξανδρος.gloss: man-defender; Alexander, the name of three Israelites and one other man

ἀληθεύω.gloss: to be true (in doctrine and profession)

ἁλιεύς.gloss: a sailor (as engaged on the salt water), i.e. (by implication) a fisher

ἁλιεύω.gloss: to be a fisher, i.e. (by implication) to fish

ἀλλά.gloss: properly, other things, i.e. (adverbially) contrariwise (in many relations)

ἀλλοτριεπίσκοπος.gloss: overseeing others' affairs, i.e. a meddler (specially, in Gentile customs)

ἀλλότριος.gloss: another's, i.e. not one's own; by extension foreign, not akin, hostile

ἅλων.gloss: a threshing-floor (as rolled hard), i.e. (figuratively) the grain (and chaff, as just threshed)

ἅμα.gloss: properly, at the "same" time, but freely used as a preposition or adverb denoting close association

ἁμαρτάνω.gloss: properly, to miss the mark (and so not share in the prize), i.e. (figuratively) to err, especially (morally) to sin

ἀμάω.gloss: properly, to collect, i.e. (by implication) reap

ἄμπελος.gloss: a vine (as coiling about a support)

ἀμφίβληστρον.gloss: a (fishing) net (as thrown about the fish)

Ἀμφίπολις.gloss: a city surrounded by a river; Amphipolis, a place in Macedonia

ἀνά.gloss: properly, up; but (by extension) used (distributively) severally, or (locally) at (etc.)

ἀναβιβάζω.gloss: to cause to go up, i.e. haul (a net)

ἀναβλέπω.gloss: to look up; by implication, to recover sight

ἀναγινώσκω.gloss: to know again, i.e. (by extension) to read

ἀνάγκη.gloss: constraint (literally or figuratively); by implication, distress

ἀνάγω.gloss: to lead up; by extension to bring out; specially, to sail away

ἀναδείκνυμι.gloss: to exhibit, i.e. (by implication) to indicate, appoint

ἀνάθεμα.gloss: a (religious) ban or (concretely) excommunicated (thing or person)

ἀναθεματίζω.gloss: to declare or vow under penalty of execration

ἀναθεωρέω.gloss: to look again (i.e. attentively) at (literally or figuratively)

ἀναιρέω.gloss: to take up, i.e. adopt; by implication, to take away (violently), i.e. abolish, murder

ἀνακαθίζω.gloss: properly, to set up, i.e. (reflexively) to sit up

ἀνακεῖμαι.gloss: to recline (as a corpse or at a meal)

ἀνακρίνω.gloss: properly, to scrutinize, i.e. (by implication) investigate, interrogate, determine

ἀνακύπτω.gloss: to unbend, i.e. rise; figuratively, be elated

ἀναλίσκω.gloss: properly, to use up, i.e. destroy

ἀναλύω.gloss: to break up, i.e. depart (literally or figuratively)

ἀνανήφω.gloss: to become sober again, i.e. (figuratively) regain (one's) senses

ἀναπαύω.gloss: (reflexively) to repose (literally or figuratively (be exempt), remain); by implication, to refresh

ἀναπίπτω.gloss: to fall back, i.e. lie down, lean back

ἀναπληρόω.gloss: to complete; by implication, to occupy, supply; figuratively, to accomplish (by coincidence ot obedience)

ἀνασκευάζω.gloss: properly, to pack up (baggage), i.e. (by implication, and figuratively) to upset

ἀνάστασις.gloss: a standing up again, i.e. (literally) a resurrection from death (individual, genitive case or by implication, (its author)), or (figuratively) a (moral) recovery (of spiritual truth)

ἀναστατόω.gloss: properly, to drive out of home, i.e. (by implication) to disturb (literally or figuratively)

ἀναστρέφω.gloss: to overturn; also to return; by implication, to busy oneself, i.e. remain, live

ἀνατίθεμαι.gloss: to set forth (for oneself), i.e propound

ἀνατολή.gloss: a rising of light, i.e. dawn (figuratively); by implication, the east (also in plural)

ἀναφαίνω.gloss: to show, i.e. (reflexively) appear, or (passively) to have pointed out

ἀνάψυξις.gloss: properly, a recovery of breath, i.e. (figuratively) revival

ἀναψύχω.gloss: properly, to cool off, i.e. (figuratively) relieve

ἀνδραποδιστής.gloss: an enslaver (as bringing men to his feet)

Ἀνδρόνικος.gloss: man of victory; Andronicos, an Israelite

ἀνεκδιήγητος.gloss: not expounded in full, i.e. indescribable

ἀνεκλάλητος.gloss: not spoken out, i.e. (by implication) unutterable

ἀνέκλειπτος.gloss: not left out, i.e. (by implication) inexhaustible

ἄνεμος.gloss: wind; (plural) by implication, (the four) quarters (of the earth)

ἀνεξερεύνητος.gloss: not searched out, i.e. (by implication) inscrutable

ἀνεξιχνίαστος.gloss: not tracked out, i.e. (by implication) untraceable

ἀνέχομαι.gloss: to hold oneself up against, i.e. (figuratively) put up with

ἀνήκω.gloss: to attain to, i.e. (figuratively) be proper

ἀνήρ.gloss: a man (properly as an individual male)

ἀνθομολογέομαι.gloss: to confess in turn, i.e. respond in praise

ἀνθύπατος.gloss: instead of the highest officer, i.e. (specially) a Roman proconsul

ἀνίημι.gloss: to let up, i.e. (literally) slacken or (figuratively) desert, desist from

ἀνίστημι.gloss: to stand up (literal or figurative, transitive or intransitive)

ἀνοίγω.gloss: to open up (literally or figuratively, in various applications)

ἀνομία.gloss: illegality, i.e. violation of law or (genitive case) wickedness

ἄνομος.gloss: lawless, i.e. (negatively) not subject to (the Jewish) law; (by implication, a Gentile), or (positively) wicked

ἀνόμως.gloss: lawlessly, i.e. (specially) not amenable to (the Jewish) law

ἀντέχομαι.gloss: to hold oneself opposite to, i.e. (by implication) adhere to; by extension to care for

ἀντί.gloss: opposite, i.e. instead or because of (rarely in addition to)

ἀντιδιατίθεμαι.gloss: to set oneself opposite, i.e. be disputatious

ἀντίδικος.gloss: an opponent (in a lawsuit); specially, Satan (as the arch-enemy)

ἀντικαθίστημι.gloss: to set down (troops) against, i.e. withstand

ἀντίκειμαι.gloss: to lie opposite, i.e. be adverse (figuratively, repugnant) to

ἀντιλαμβάνομαι.gloss: to take hold of in turn, i.e. succor; also to participate

Ἀντιοχεύς.gloss: an Antiochian or inhabitant of Antiochia

ἀντιτάσσομαι.gloss: to range oneself against, i.e. oppose

ἀντλέω.gloss: to bale up (properly, bilge water), i.e. dip water (with a bucket, pitcher, etc.)

ἀνυπότακτος.gloss: unsubdued, i.e. insubordinate (in fact or temper)

ἀνώγεον.gloss: above the ground, i.e. (properly) the second floor of a building; used for a dome or a balcony on the upper story

ἄνωθεν.gloss: from above; by analogy, from the first; by implication, anew

ἀνώτερος.gloss: upper, i.e. (neuter as adverb) to a more conspicuous place, in a former part of the book

ἄξιος.gloss: deserving, comparable or suitable (as if drawing praise)

ἀπάγχομαι.gloss: to strangle oneself off (i.e. to death)

ἀπαλγέω.gloss: to grieve out, i.e. become apathetic

ἀπαλλάσσω.gloss: to change away, i.e. release, (reflexively) remove

ἀπαλλοτριόω.gloss: to estrange away, i.e. (passively and figuratively) to be non-participant

ἅπαξ.gloss: one (or a single) time (numerically or conclusively)

ἀπαρνέομαι.gloss: to deny utterly, i.e. disown, abstain

ἀπαρχή.gloss: a beginning of sacrifice, i.e. the (Jewish) first-fruit (figuratively)

ἀπειλέω.gloss: to menace; by implication, to forbid

ἀπειπόμην.gloss: to say off for oneself, i.e. disown

ἀπεκδύομαι.gloss: to divest wholly oneself, or (for oneself) despoil

ἀπελεύθερος.gloss: one freed away, i.e. a freedman

ἀπελπίζω.gloss: to hope out, i.e. fully expect

ἀπέναντι.gloss: from in front, i.e. opposite, before or against

ἀπέρχομαι.gloss: to go off (i.e. depart), aside (i.e. apart) or behind (i.e. follow), literally or figuratively

ἀπέχομαι.gloss: to hold oneself off, i.e. refrain

ἀπέχω.gloss: (actively) to have out, i.e. receive in full; (intransitively) to keep (oneself) away, i.e. be distant (literally or figuratively)

ἀπιστέω.gloss: to be unbelieving, i.e. (transitively) disbelieve, or (by implication) disobey

ἀπιστία.gloss: faithlessness, i.e. (negatively) disbelief (lack of Christian faith), or (positively) unfaithfulness (disobedience)

ἄπιστος.gloss: (actively) disbelieving, i.e. without Christian faith (specially, a heathen); (passively) untrustworthy (person), or incredible (thing)

ἁπλότης.gloss: singleness, i.e. (subjectively) sincerity (without dissimulation or self-seeking), or (objectively) generosity (copious bestowal)

ἁπλοῦς.gloss: properly, folded together, i.e. single (figuratively, clear)

ἀπό.gloss: "off," i.e. away (from something near), in various senses (of place, time, or relation; literal or figurative)

ἀποβαίνω.gloss: literally, to disembark; figuratively, to eventuate

ἀποβάλλω.gloss: to throw off; figuratively, to lose

ἀποβλέπω.gloss: to look away from everything else, i.e. (figuratively) intently regard

ἀπόβλητος.gloss: cast off, i.e. (figuratively) such as to be rejected

ἀπογραφή.gloss: an enrollment; by implication, an assessment

ἀπογράφω.gloss: to write off (a copy or list), i.e. enrol

ἀποδείκνυμι.gloss: to show off, i.e. exhibit; figuratively, to demonstrate, i.e. accredit

ἀποδέχομαι.gloss: to take fully, i.e. welcome (persons), approve (things)

ἀποδημέω.gloss: to go abroad, i.e. visit a foreign land

ἀπόδημος.gloss: absent from one's own people, i.e. a foreign traveller

ἀποδίδωμι.gloss: to give away, i.e. up, over, back, etc. (in various applications)

ἀποδιορίζω.gloss: to disjoin (by a boundary, figuratively, a party)

ἀποδοκιμάζω.gloss: to disapprove, i.e. (by implication) to repudiate

ἀποκαθίστημι.gloss: to reconstitute (in health, home or organization)

ἀποκαλύπτω.gloss: to take off the cover, i.e. disclose

ἀπόκειμαι.gloss: to be reserved; figuratively, to await

ἀποκόπτω.gloss: to amputate; reflexively (by irony) to mutilate (the privy parts)

ἀποκρίνομαι.gloss: to conclude for oneself, i.e. (by implication) to respond; by Hebraism (compare ) to begin to speak (where an address is expected)

ἀποκρύπτω.gloss: to conceal away (i.e. fully); figuratively, to keep secret

ἀποκτείνω.gloss: to kill outright; figuratively, to destroy

ἀποκυέω.gloss: to breed forth, i.e. (by transference) to generate (figuratively)

ἀπολαμβάνω.gloss: to receive (specially, in full, or as a host); also to take aside

ἀπολείπω.gloss: to leave behind (passively, remain); by implication, to forsake

ἀπόλλυμι.gloss: to destroy fully (reflexively, to perish, or lose), literally or figuratively

ἀπολογέομαι.gloss: to give an account (legal plea) of oneself, i.e. exculpate (self)

ἀπολούω.gloss: to wash fully, i.e. (figuratively) have remitted (reflexively)

ἀπολύτρωσις.gloss: (the act) ransom in full, i.e. (figuratively) riddance, or (specially) Christian salvation

ἀπολύω.gloss: to free fully, i.e. (literally) relieve, release, dismiss (reflexively, depart), or (figuratively) let die, pardon or (specially) divorce

ἀπονίπτω.gloss: to wash off (reflexively, one's own hands symbolically)

ἀποπλανάω.gloss: to lead astray (figuratively); passively, to stray (from truth)

ἀπορέω.gloss: to have no way out, i.e. be at a loss (mentally)

ἀπορφανίζω.gloss: to bereave wholly, i.e. (figuratively) separate (from intercourse)

ἀποσπάω.gloss: to drag forth, i.e. (literally) unsheathe (a sword), or relatively (with a degree of force implied) retire (personally or factiously)

ἀποστέλλω.gloss: set apart, i.e. (by implication) to send out (properly, on a mission) literally or figuratively

ἀπόστολος.gloss: a delegate; specially, an ambassador of the Gospel; officially a commissioner of Christ ("apostle") (with miraculous powers)

ἀποστοματίζω.gloss: to speak off-hand (properly, dictate), i.e. to catechize (in an invidious manner)

ἀποστρέφω.gloss: to turn away or back (literally or figuratively)

ἀποτάσσομαι.gloss: literally, to say adieu (by departing or dismissing); figuratively, to renounce

ἀπόχρησις.gloss: the act of using up, i.e. consumption

ἀποχωρίζω.gloss: to rend apart; reflexively, to separate

ἀπρόσκοπος.gloss: actively, inoffensive, i.e. not leading into sin; passively, faultless, i.e. not led into sin

ἀπροσωπολήπτως.gloss: in a way not accepting the person, i.e. impartially

ἅπτομαι.gloss: properly, to attach oneself to, i.e. to touch (in many implied relations)

ἅπτω.gloss: properly, to fasten to, i.e. (specially) to set on fire

ἀπωθέομαι.gloss: to push off, figuratively, to reject

ἀπώλεια.gloss: ruin or loss (physical, spiritual or eternal)

ἀρά.gloss: properly, prayer (as lifted to Heaven), i.e. (by implication) imprecation

ἄρα.gloss: a particle denoting an inference more or less decisive (as follows)

Ἄραψ.gloss: an Arab or native of Arabia

ἀργέω.gloss: to be idle, i.e. (figuratively) to delay

ἀργός.gloss: inactive, i.e. unemployed; (by implication) lazy, useless

ἀργύριον.gloss: silvery, i.e. (by implication) cash; specially, a silverling (i.e. drachma or shekel)

ἄργυρος.gloss: silver (the metal, in the articles or coin)

Ἄρειος Πάγος.gloss: rock of Ares, a place in Athens

Ἀρεοπαγίτης.gloss: an Areopagite or member of the court held on Mars' Hill

ἀρέσκω.gloss: to be agreeable (or by implication, to seek to be so)

ἀρέτη.gloss: properly, manliness (valor), i.e. excellence (intrinsic or attributed)

ἄριστον.gloss: the best meal (or breakfast; perhaps from ("early")), i.e. luncheon

ἀρκέω.gloss: properly, to ward off, i.e. (by implication) to avail (figuratively, be satisfactory)

ἅρμα.gloss: a chariot (as raised or fitted together (compare ))

ἁρμόζω.gloss: to joint, i.e. (figuratively) to woo (reflexively, to betroth)

ἀρνέομαι.gloss: to contradict, i.e. disavow, reject, abnegate

ἄῤῥαφος.gloss: unsewed, i.e. of a single piece

Ἀρτεμάς.gloss: gift of Artemis; Artemas (or Artemidorus), a Christian

Ἄρτεμις.gloss: prompt; Artemis, the name of a Grecian goddess borrowed by the Asiatics for one of their deities

ἀρτέμων.gloss: properly, something ready (or else more remotely from (compare ); something hung up), i.e. (specially) the topsail (rather foresail or jib) of a vessel

ἀρτιγέννητος.gloss: just born, i.e. (figuratively) a young convert

ἀρτύω.gloss: to prepare, i.e. spice (with stimulating condiments)

ἀρχή.gloss: (properly abstract) a commencement, or (concretely) chief (in various applications of order, time, place, or rank)

ἀρχιερεύς.gloss: the high-priest (literally, of the Jews, typically, Christ); by extension a chief priest

ἄρχω.gloss: to be first (in political rank or power)

ἀσεβέω.gloss: to be (by implied act) impious or wicked

ἀσεβής.gloss: irreverent, i.e. (by extension) impious or wicked

ἀσθένεια.gloss: feebleness (of mind or body); by implication, malady; morally, frailty

ἀσθενής.gloss: strengthless (in various applications, literal, figurative and moral)

Ἀσία.gloss: Asia, i.e. Asia Minor, or (usually) only its western shore

Ἀσιανός.gloss: an Asian (i.e. Asiatic) or an inhabitant of Asia

Ἀσιάρχης.gloss: an Asiarch or president of the public festivities in a city of Asia Minor

ἀσκέω.gloss: to elaborate, i.e. (figuratively) train (by implication, strive)

ἀσκός.gloss: a leathern (or skin) bag used as a bottle

ἀσπάζομαι.gloss: to enfold in the arms, i.e. (by implication) to salute, (figuratively) to welcome

ἀσπασμός.gloss: a greeting (in person or by letter)

ἄσπονδος.gloss: literally, without libation (which usually accompanied a treaty), i.e. (by implication) truceless

ἀστήρ.gloss: a star (as strown over the sky), literally or figuratively

ἀστοχέω.gloss: to miss the mark, i.e. (figuratively) deviate from truth

ἄστρον.gloss: properly, a constellation; put for a single star (natural or artificial)

ἀσύνθετος.gloss: properly, not agreed, i.e. treacherous to compacts

ἀσχημοσύνη.gloss: an indecency; by implication, the pudenda

εὐψυχέω.gloss: to be in good spirits, i.e. feel encouraged

Ἔφεσος.gloss: Ephesus, a city of Asia Minor

ἐφικνέομαι.gloss: to arrive upon, i.e. extend to

ἐφίστημι.gloss: to stand upon, i.e. be present (in various applications, friendly or otherwise, usually literal)

ἔχθρα.gloss: hostility; by implication, a reason for opposition

ἐχθρός.gloss: hateful (passively, odious, or actively, hostile); usually as a noun, an adversary (especially Satan)

ἔχω.gloss: to hold (used in very various applications, literally or figuratively, direct or remote; such as possession; ability, contiuity, relation, or condition)

ζεῦγος.gloss: a couple, i.e. a team (of oxen yoked together) or brace (of birds tied together)

ζήτημα.gloss: a search (properly concretely), i.e. (in words) a debate

ζήτησις.gloss: a searching (properly, the act), i.e. a dispute or its theme

ζώνη.gloss: a belt; by implication, a pocket

ζώννυμι.gloss: to bind about (especially with a belt)

ζῶον.gloss: a live thing, i.e. an animal

ἡγεμών.gloss: a leader, i.e. chief person (or figuratively, place) of a province

ἡγέομαι.gloss: to lead, i.e. command (with official authority); figuratively, to deem, i.e. consider

ἥκω.gloss: to arrive, i.e. be present (literally or figuratively)

ἡλίκος.gloss: as big as, i.e. (interjectively) how much

ἡμέρα.gloss: day, i.e. (literally) the time space between dawn and dark, or the whole 24 hours (but several days were usually reckoned by the Jews as inclusive of the parts of both extremes); figuratively, a period (always defined more or less clearly by the context)

ἤπιος.gloss: properly, affable, i.e. mild or kind

Ἡρώδης.gloss: heroic; Herod, the name of four Jewish kings

Ἡρωδιάς.gloss: Herodias, a woman of the Heodian family

ἡσυχία.gloss: (as noun) stillness, i.e. desistance from bustle or language

ἡσύχιος.gloss: properly, keeping one's seat (sedentary), i.e. (by implication) still (undisturbed, undisturbing)

ἥττημα.gloss: a deterioration, i.e. (objectively) failure or (subjectively) loss

ἥττον.gloss: worse (as noun); by implication, less (as adverb)

ἠχέω.gloss: to make a loud noise, i.e. reverberate

ἦχος.gloss: a loud or confused noise ("echo"), i.e. roar; figuratively, a rumor

θάνατος.gloss: (properly, an adjective used as a noun) death (literally or figuratively)

θάπτω.gloss: to celebrate funeral rites, i.e. inter

θαῦμα.gloss: wonder (properly concrete; but by implication, abstract)

θαυμάζω.gloss: to wonder; by implication, to admire

θαυμάσιος.gloss: wondrous, i.e. (neuter as noun) a miracle

θεάομαι.gloss: to look closely at, i.e. (by implication) perceive (literally or figuratively); by extension to visit

θέατρον.gloss: a place for public show ("theatre"), i.e. general audience-room; by implication, a show itself (figuratively)

θεμελιόω.gloss: to lay a basis for, i.e. (literally) erect, or (figuratively) consolidate

θεός.gloss: a deity, especially (with ) the supreme Divinity; figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very

Θεόφιλος.gloss: friend of God; Theophilus, a Christian

θεραπεία.gloss: attendance (specially, medical, i.e. cure); figuratively and collectively, domestics

θεραπεύω.gloss: to wait upon menially, i.e. (figuratively) to adore (God), or (specially) to relieve (of disease)

Θεσσαλονικεύς.gloss: a Thessalonican, i.e. inhabitant of Thessalonice

Θεσσαλονίκη.gloss: Thessalonice, a place in Asia Minor

θεωρέω.gloss: to be a spectator of, i.e. discern, (literally, figuratively (experience) or intensively (acknowledge))

θηρεύω.gloss: to hunt (an animal), i.e. (figuratively) to carp at

θηριομαχέω.gloss: to be a beast-fighter (in the gladiatorial show), i.e. (figuratively) to encounter (furious men)

θησαυρίζω.gloss: to amass or reserve (literally or figuratively)

θησαυρός.gloss: a deposit, i.e. wealth (literally or figuratively)

θορυβέω.gloss: to be in tumult, i.e. disturb, clamor

θρησκός.gloss: ceremonious in worship (as demonstrative), i.e. pious

θριαμβεύω.gloss: and a derivative of (meaning a noisy iambus, sung in honor of Bacchus); to make an acclamatory procession, i.e. (figuratively) to conquer or (by Hebraism) to give victory

θροέω.gloss: to clamor, i.e. (by implication) to frighten

θυγάτηρ.gloss: a female child, or (by Hebraism) descendant (or inhabitant)

θυμίαμα.gloss: an aroma, i.e. fragrant powder burnt in religious service; by implication, the burning itself

θυμιαστήριον.gloss: a place of fumigation, i.e. the alter of incense (in the Temple)

θυμιάω.gloss: to fumigate, i.e. offer aromatic fumes

θυμομαχέω.gloss: to be in a furious fight, i.e. (figuratively) to be exasperated

θυμόω.gloss: to put in a passion, i.e. enrage

θύρα.gloss: a portal or entrance (the opening or the closure, literally or figuratively)

θυσία.gloss: sacrifice (the act or the victim, literally or figuratively)

θυσιαστήριον.gloss: a place of sacrifice, i.e. an altar (special or genitive case, literal or figurative)

θύω.gloss: properly, to rush (breathe hard, blow, smoke), i.e. (by implication) to sacrifice (properly, by fire, but genitive case); by extension to immolate (slaughter for any purpose)

Ἰάκωβος.gloss: Jacobus, the name of three Israelites

Ἰάσων.gloss: about to cure; Jason, a Christian

ἴδε.gloss: used as an interjection to denote surprise; lo!

ἴδιος.gloss: pertaining to self, i.e. one's own; by implication, private or separate

ἰδιώτης.gloss: a private person, i.e. (by implication) an ignoramus (compare "idiot")

Ἱεράπολις.gloss: holy city; Hierapolis, a place in Asia Minor

ἱεράτευμα.gloss: the priestly fraternity, i.e. sacerdotal order (figuratively)

ἱερατεύω.gloss: to be a priest, i.e. perform his functions

ἱερόν.gloss: a sacred place, i.e. the entire precincts (whereas denotes the central sanctuary itself) of the Temple (at Jerusalem or elsewhere)

Ἱεροσόλυμα.gloss: Hierosolyma (i.e. Jerushalaim), the capitol of Palestine

Ἱεροσολυμίτης.gloss: a Hierosolymite, i.e. inhabitant of Hierosolyma

ἱερουργέω.gloss: to be a temple-worker, i.e. officiate as a priest (figuratively)

ἱερωσύνη.gloss: sacredness, i.e. (by implication) the priestly office

ἱκανός.gloss: competent (as if coming in season), i.e. ample (in amount) or fit (in character)

Ἰκόνιον.gloss: image-like; Iconium, a place in Asia Minor

ἱλαρός.gloss: propitious or merry ("hilarious"), i.e. prompt or willing

ἱλάσκομαι.gloss: to conciliate, i.e. (transitively) to atone for (sin), or (intransitively) be propitious

ἱλαστήριον.gloss: an expiatory (place or thing), i.e. (concretely) an atoning victim, or (specially) the lid of the Ark (in the Temple)

ἵλεως.gloss: cheerful (as attractive), i.e. propitious; adverbially (by Hebraism) God be gracious!, i.e. (in averting some calamity) far be it

ἱμάς.gloss: a strap, i.e. (specially) the tie (of a sandal) or the lash (of a scourge)

ἵνα.gloss: in order that (denoting the purpose or the result)

ἰός.gloss: rust (as if emitted by metals); also venom (as emitted by serpents)

Ἰουδά.gloss: Judah (i.e. Jehudah or Juttah), a part of (or place in) Palestine

Ἰουδαία.gloss: the Judæan land (i.e. Judæa), a region of Palestine

Ἰουδαϊκῶς.gloss: Judaïcally or in a manner resembling a Judæan

Ἰουδαϊσμός.gloss: "Judaism", i.e. the Jewish faith and usages

Ἰούδας.gloss: Judas (i.e. Jehudah), the name of ten Israelites; also of the posterity of one of them and its region

ἱππεύς.gloss: an equestrian, i.e. member of a cavalry corps

Ἰσραήλ.gloss: Israel (i.e. Jisrael), the adopted name of Jacob, including his descendants (literally or figuratively)

Ἰσραηλίτης.gloss: an "Israelite", i.e. descendant of Israel (literally or figuratively)

ἵστημι.gloss: to stand (transitively or intransitively), used in various applications (literally or figuratively)

ἱστορέω.gloss: to be knowing (learned), i.e. (by implication) to visit for information (interview)

ἰσχύς.gloss: compare , a form of ); forcefulness (literally or figuratively)

ἰσχύω.gloss: to have (or exercise) force (literally or figuratively)

Ἰωάννης.gloss: Joannes (i.e. Jochanan), the name of four Israelites

Ἰωνᾶς.gloss: Jonas (i.e. Jonah), the name of two Israelites

Ἰωσῆς.gloss: Joses, the name of two Israelites

Ἰωσήφ.gloss: Joseph, the name of seven Israelites

κἀγώ.gloss: so also the dative case , and accusative case and (or also, even, etc.) I, (to) me

καθά.gloss: according to which things, i.e. just as

καθαιρέω.gloss: to lower (or with violence) demolish (literally or figuratively)

καθαίρω.gloss: to cleanse, i.e. (specially) to prune; figuratively, to expiate

καθαρισμός.gloss: a washing off, i.e. (ceremonially) ablution, (morally) expiation

καθεξῆς.gloss: thereafter, i.e. consecutively; as a noun (by ellipsis of noun) a subsequent person or time

καθεύδω.gloss: to lie down to rest, i.e. (by implication) to fall asleep (literally or figuratively)

καθήκω.gloss: to reach to, i.e. (neuter of present active participle, figuratively as adjective) becoming

κάθημαι.gloss: and (to sit; akin to the base of ); to sit down; figuratively, to remain, reside

καθίζω.gloss: to seat down, i.e. set (figuratively, appoint); intransitively, to sit (down); figuratively, to settle (hover, dwell)

καθίστημι.gloss: to place down (permanently), i.e. (figuratively) to designate, constitute, convoy

καθό.gloss: according to which thing, i.e. precisely as, in proportion as

καθοπλίζω.gloss: and ; to equip fully with armor

καθοράω.gloss: to behold fully, i.e. (figuratively) distinctly apprehend

καθότι.gloss: and and ; according to which certain thing, i.e. as far (or inasmuch) as

καί.gloss: and, also, even, so then, too, etc.; often used in connection (or composition) with other particles or small words
derivedForms: ὁ ὢν καί ὁ ἦν καί ὁ ἐρχόμενος

καινός.gloss: new (especially in freshness; while is properly so with respect to age

καίπερ.gloss: and indeed, i.e. nevertheless or notwithstanding

καιρός.gloss: an occasion, i.e. set or proper time

Καῖσαρ.gloss: Cæsar, a title of the Roman emperor

Καισάρεια.gloss: Cæsaria, the name of two places in Palestine

καίτοιγε.gloss: and yet indeed, i.e. although really

καίω.gloss: to set on fire, i.e. kindle or (by implication) consume

κακία.gloss: badness, i.e. (subjectively) depravity, or (actively) malignity, or (passively) trouble

κακοποιέω.gloss: to be a bad-doer, i.e. (objectively) to injure, or (genitive) to sin

κακός.gloss: worthless (intrinsically, such; whereas properly refers to effects), i.e. (subjectively) depraved, or (objectively) injurious

καλάμη.gloss: a stalk of grain, i.e. (collectively) stubble

κάλαμος.gloss: a reed (the plant or its stem, or that of a similar plant); by implication, a pen

καλέω.gloss: to "call" (properly, aloud, but used in a variety of applications, directly or otherwise)

καλλιέλαιος.gloss: a cultivated olive tree, i.e. a domesticated or improved one

Καλοὶ Λιμένες.gloss: Good Harbors, i.e. Fairhaven, a bay of Crete

καλοποιέω.gloss: to do well, i.e. live virtuously

καλός.gloss: properly, beautiful, but chiefly (figuratively) good (literally or morally), i.e. valuable or virtuous (for appearance or use, and thus distinguished from , which is properly intrinsic)

καμμύω.gloss: to shut down, i.e. close the eyes

καρδία.gloss: the heart, i.e. (figuratively) the thoughts or feelings (mind); also (by analogy) the middle

καταβιβάζω.gloss: to cause to go down, i.e. precipitate

καταβολή.gloss: a deposition, i.e. founding; figuratively, conception

καταβραβεύω.gloss: to award the price against, i.e. (figuratively) to defraud (of salvation)

καταγινώσκω.gloss: to note against, i.e. find fault with

κατάγνυμι.gloss: to rend in pieces, i.e. crack apart

κατάγω.gloss: to lead down; specially, to moor a vessel

καταγωνίζομαι.gloss: to struggle against, i.e. (by implication) to overcome

καταδέω.gloss: to tie down, i.e. bandage (a wound)

καταδικάζω.gloss: to adjudge against, i.e. pronounce guilty

καταδιώκω.gloss: to hunt down, i.e. search for

καταδυναστεύω.gloss: to exercise dominion against, i.e. oppress

καταισχύνω.gloss: to shame down, i.e. disgrace or (by implication) put to the blush

κατάκειμαι.gloss: to lie down, i.e. (by implication) be sick; specially, to recline at a meal

κατακλείω.gloss: to shut down (in a dungeon), i.e. incarcerate

κατακληροδοτέω.gloss: to be a giver of lots to each, i.e. (by implication) to apportion an estate

κατακλίνω.gloss: to recline down, i.e. (specially) to take a place at table

κατακλύζω.gloss: to dash (wash) down, i.e. (by implication) to deluge

κατακυριεύω.gloss: to lord against, i.e. control, subjugate

καταλαλέω.gloss: to be a traducer, i.e. to slander

καταλαμβάνω.gloss: to take eagerly, i.e. seize, possess, etc. (literally or figuratively)

καταλέγω.gloss: to lay down, i.e. (figuratively) to enrol

κατάλειμμα.gloss: a remainder, i.e. (by implication) a few

καταλείπω.gloss: to leave down, i.e. behind; by implication, to abandon, have remaining

καταλιθάζω.gloss: to stone down, i.e. to death

καταλλαγή.gloss: exchange (figuratively, adjustment), i.e. restoration to (the divine) favor

καταλλάσσω.gloss: to change mutually, i.e. (figuratively) to compound a difference

κατάλοιπος.gloss: left down (behind), i.e remaining (plural the rest)

κατάλυμα.gloss: properly, a dissolution (breaking up of a journey), i.e. (by implication) a lodging-place

καταλύω.gloss: to loosen down (disintegrate), i.e. (by implication) to demolish (literally or figuratively); specially (compare ) to halt for the night

καταμανθάνω.gloss: to learn thoroughly, i.e. (by implication) to note carefully

καταμόνας.gloss: according to sole places, i.e. (adverbially) separately

καταναρκάω.gloss: to grow utterly torpid, i.e. (by implication) slothful (figuratively, expensive)

κατανεύω.gloss: to nod down (towards), i.e. (by analogy) to make signs to

καταντάω.gloss: to meet against, i.e. arrive at (literally or figuratively)

κατάνυξις.gloss: a prickling (sensation, as of the limbs asleep), i.e. (by implication, (perhaps by some confusion with or even with )) stupor (lethargy)

κατανύσσω.gloss: to pierce thoroughly, i.e. (figuratively) to agitate violently ("sting to the quick")

καταπατέω.gloss: to trample down; figuratively, to reject with disdain

καταπέτασμα.gloss: something spread thoroughly, i.e. (specially) the door screen (to the Most Holy Place) in the Jewish Temple

καταπίνω.gloss: to drink down, i.e. gulp entire (literally or figuratively)

καταπλέω.gloss: to sail down upon a place, i.e. to land at

καταπονέω.gloss: to labor down, i.e. wear with toil (figuratively, harass)

καταράομαι.gloss: to execrate; by analogy, to doom

καταργέω.gloss: to be (render) entirely idle (useless), literally or figuratively

καταρτίζω.gloss: to complete thoroughly, i.e. repair (literally or figuratively) or adjust

κατασείω.gloss: to sway downward, i.e. make a signal

κατασκευάζω.gloss: to prepare thoroughly (properly, by external equipment; whereas refers rather to internal fitness); by implication, to construct, create

κατασκηνόω.gloss: to camp down, i.e. haunt; figuratively, to remain

κατασκοπέω.gloss: to be a sentinel, i.e. to inspect insidiously

κατασοφίζομαι.gloss: to be crafty against, i.e. circumvent

κατάστημα.gloss: properly, a position or condition, i.e. (subjectively) demeanor

καταστρέφω.gloss: to turn upside down, i.e. upset

καταστροφή.gloss: an overturn ("catastrophe"), i.e. demolition; figuratively, apostasy

σαίνω.gloss: to wag (as a dog its tail fawningly), i.e. (generally) to shake (figuratively, disturb)

σαλεύω.gloss: to waver, i.e. agitate, rock, topple or (by implication) destroy; figuratively, to disturb, incite

σαλπίζω.gloss: to trumpet, i.e. sound a blast (literally or figuratively)

Σαμάρεια.gloss: Samaria (i.e. Shomeron), a city and region of Palestine

Σαμαρείτης.gloss: a Samarite, i.e. inhabitant of Samaria

Σαμαρεῖτις.gloss: a Samaritess, i.e. woman of Samaria

Σαμοθρᾴκη.gloss: Samo-thrace (Samos of Thrace), an island in the Mediterranean

Σάμος.gloss: Samus, an island of the Mediterranean

σάρδινος.gloss: sardine ( being implied), i.e. a gem, so called

σάρδιος.gloss: sardian (3037 being implied), i.e. (as noun) the gem so called

σαρδόνυξ.gloss: a "sardonyx", i.e. the gem so called

σεισμός.gloss: a commotion, i.e. (of the air) a gale, (of the ground) an earthquake

Σιδώνιος.gloss: a Sidonian, i.e. inhabitant of Sidon

Σίμων.gloss: Simon (i.e. Shimon), the name of nine Israelites

σιτόμετρον.gloss: a grain-measure, i.e. (by implication) ration (allowance of food)

σκανδαλίζω.gloss: to entrap, i.e. trip up (figuratively, stumble (transitively) or entice to sin, apostasy or displeasure)

σκάνδαλον.gloss: a trap-stick (bent sapling), i.e. snare (figuratively, cause of displeasure or sin)

σκέλος.gloss: through the idea of leanness); the leg (as lank)

σκεῦος.gloss: a vessel, implement, equipment or apparatus (literally or figuratively [specially, a wife as contributing to the usefulness of the husband])

σκηνή.gloss: a tent or cloth hut (literally or figuratively)

σκηνοπηγία.gloss: the Festival of Tabernacles (so called from the custom of erecting booths for temporary homes)

σκῆνος.gloss: a hut or temporary residence, i.e. (figuratively) the human body (as the abode of the spirit)

σκηνόω.gloss: to tent or encamp, i.e. (figuratively) to occupy (as a mansion) or (specially), to reside (as God did in the Tabernacle of old, a symbol of protection and communion)

σκήνωμα.gloss: an encampment, i.e. (figuratively) the Temple (as God's residence), the body (as a tenement for the soul)

σκιά.gloss: "shade" or a shadow (literally or figuratively (darkness of error or an adumbration))

σκληρός.gloss: dry, i.e. hard or tough (figuratively, harsh, severe)

σκόλοψ.gloss: withered at the front, i.e. a point or prickle (figuratively, a bodily annoyance or disability)

σκοπέω.gloss: to take aim at (spy), i.e. (figuratively) regard

σκορπίζω.gloss: to dissipate, i.e. (figuratively) put to flight, waste, be liberal

σκοτόω.gloss: to obscure or blind (literally or figuratively)

σκύβαλον.gloss: what is thrown to the dogs, i.e. refuse (ordure)

σκυθρωπός.gloss: angry-visaged, i.e. gloomy or affecting a mournful appearance

σκῦλον.gloss: something stripped (as a hide), i.e. booty

σμάραγδος.gloss: the emerald or green gem so called

Σμύρνα.gloss: Smyrna, a place in Asia Minor

σμυρνίζω.gloss: to tincture with myrrh, i.e. embitter (as a narcotic)

σορός.gloss: a funereal receptacle (urn, coffin), i.e. (by analogy) a bier

σοφία.gloss: wisdom (higher or lower, worldly or spiritual)

σπαράσσω.gloss: apparently strengthened from through the idea of spasmodic contraction); to mangle, i.e. convluse with epilepsy

σπαργανόω.gloss: to swathe (an infant after the Oriental custom)

σπεῖρα.gloss: a coil (spira, "spire"), i.e. (figuratively) a mass of men (a Roman military cohort; also (by analogy) a squad of Levitical janitors)

σπείρω.gloss: to scatter, i.e. sow (literally or figuratively)

σπιλόω.gloss: to stain or soil (literally or figuratively)

σπίλος.gloss: a stain or blemish, i.e. (figuratively) defect, disgrace

σπλαγχνίζομαι.gloss: to have the bowels yearn, i.e. (figuratively) feel sympathy, to pity

σπλάγχνον.gloss: an intestine (plural); figuratively, pity or sympathy

σπόριμος.gloss: sown, i.e. (neuter plural) a planted field

σπουδαιοτέρως.gloss: more speedily, i.e. sooner than otherwise

στάδιον.gloss: a stade or certain measure of distance; by implication, a stadium or race-course

στάσις.gloss: a standing (properly, the act), i.e. (by analogy) position (existence); by implication, a popular uprising; figuratively, controversy

στατήρ.gloss: a stander (standard of value), i.e. (specially), a stater or certain coin

σταυρός.gloss: a stake or post (as set upright), i.e. (specially), a pole or cross (as an instrument of capital punishment); figuratively, exposure to death, i.e. self-denial; by implication, the atonement of Christ

σταυρόω.gloss: to impale on the cross; figuratively, to extinguish (subdue) passion or selfishness

σταφυλή.gloss: a cluster of grapes (as if intertwined)

στάχυς.gloss: a head of grain (as standing out from the stalk)

στέγω.gloss: to roof over, i.e. (figuratively) to cover with silence (endure patiently)

στέλλω.gloss: properly, to set fast ("stall"), i.e. (figuratively) to repress (reflexively, abstain from associating with)

στενάζω.gloss: to make (intransitively, be) in straits, i.e. (by implication) to sigh, murmur, pray inaudibly

στενοχωρέω.gloss: to hem in closely, i.e. (figuratively) cramp

στερεός.gloss: stiff, i.e. solid, stable (literally or figuratively)

στερεόω.gloss: to solidify, i.e. confirm (literally or figuratively)

στέφανος.gloss: a chaplet (as a badge of royalty, a prize in the public games or a symbol of honor generally; but more conspicuous and elaborate than the simple fillet, ), literally or figuratively

στεφανόω.gloss: to adorn with an honorary wreath (literally or figuratively)

στήκω.gloss: to be stationary, i.e. (figuratively) to persevere

στηρίζω.gloss: to set fast, i.e. (literally) to turn resolutely in a certain direction, or (figuratively) to confirm

στίγμα.gloss: a mark incised or punched (for recognition of ownership), i.e. (figuratively) scar of service

στιγμή.gloss: a point of time, i.e. an instant

στοιχεῖον.gloss: something orderly in arrangement, i.e. (by implication) a serial (basal, fundamental, initial) constituent (literally), proposition (figuratively)

στοιχέω.gloss: to march in (military) rank (keep step), i.e. (figuratively) to conform to virtue and piety

στολή.gloss: equipment, i.e. (specially), a "stole" or long-fitting gown (as a mark of dignity)

στόμα.gloss: the mouth (as if a gash in the face); by implication, language (and its relations); figuratively, an opening (in the earth); specially, the front or edge (of a weapon)

στρατεία.gloss: military service, i.e. (figuratively) the apostolic career (as one of hardship and danger)

στράτευμα.gloss: an armament, i.e. (by implication) a body of troops (more or less extensive or systematic)

στρατηγός.gloss: a general, i.e. (by implication or analogy) a (military) governor (prætor), the chief (præfect) of the (Levitical) temple-wardens

στρατιώτης.gloss: a camper-out, i.e. a (common) warrior (literally or figuratively)

στρατοπεδάρχης.gloss: a ruler of an army, i.e. (specially), a Prætorian præfect

στρατόπεδον.gloss: a camping-ground, i.e. (by implication) a body of troops

στρεβλόω.gloss: to wrench, i.e. (specially), to torture (by the rack), but only figuratively, to pervert

στρέφω.gloss: to twist, i.e. turn quite around or reverse (literally or figuratively)

στρώννυμι.gloss: to "strew," i.e. spread (as a carpet or couch)

στυγνάζω.gloss: to render gloomy, i.e. (by implication) glower (be overcast with clouds, or sombreness of speech)

συγγενής.gloss: a relative (by blood); by extension, a fellow countryman

συγκαθίζω.gloss: to give (or take) a seat in company with

συγκακοπαθέω.gloss: to suffer hardship in company with

συγκακουχέω.gloss: to maltreat in company with, i.e. (passively) endure persecution together

συγκάμπτω.gloss: to bend together, i.e. (figuratively) to afflict

συγκατάθεσις.gloss: a deposition (of sentiment) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) accord with

συγκατατίθεμαι.gloss: to deposit (one's vote or opinion) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to accord with

συγκαταψηφίζω.gloss: to count down in company with, i.e. enroll among

συγκεράννυμι.gloss: to commingle, i.e. (figuratively) to combine or assimilate

συγκινέω.gloss: to move together, i.e. (specially), to excite as a mass (to sedition)

συγκλείω.gloss: to shut together, i.e. include or (figuratively) embrace in a common subjection to

συγκληρονόμος.gloss: a co-heir, i.e. (by analogy) participant in common

συγκοινωνέω.gloss: to share in company with, i.e. co-participate in

συγκομίζω.gloss: to convey together, i.e. collect or bear away in company with others

συγκρίνω.gloss: to judge of one thing in connection with another, i.e. combine (spiritual ideas with appropriate expressions) or collate (one person with another by way of contrast or resemblance)

συγκύπτω.gloss: to stoop altogether, i.e. be completely overcome by

συγχράομαι.gloss: to use jointly, i.e. (by implication) to hold intercourse in common

συζάω.gloss: to continue to live in common with, i.e. co-survive (literally or figuratively)

συζεύγνυμι.gloss: to yoke together, i.e. (figuratively) conjoin (in marriage)

συζητέω.gloss: to investigate jointly, i.e. discuss, controvert, cavil

συλλυπέω.gloss: to afflict jointly, i.e. (passive) sorrow at (on account of) someone

συμβαίνω.gloss: to walk (figuratively, transpire) together, i.e. concur (take place)

συμβούλιον.gloss: advisement; specially, a deliberative body, i.e. the provincial assessors or lay-court

Συμεών.gloss: Symeon (i.e. Shimon), the name of five Israelites

συμμαρτυρέω.gloss: to testify jointly, i.e. corroborate by (concurrent) evidence

συμμερίζομαι.gloss: to share jointly, i.e. participate in

συμμορφόω.gloss: to render like, i.e. (figuratively) to assimilate

συμπαθέω.gloss: to feel "sympathy" with, i.e. (by implication) to commiserate

συμπαθής.gloss: having a fellow-feeling ("sympathetic"), i.e. (by implication) mutually commiserative

συμπαραγίνομαι.gloss: to be present together, i.e. to convene; by implication, to appear in aid

συμπαραμένω.gloss: to remain in company, i.e. still live

συμπάρειμι.gloss: to be at hand together, i.e. now present

συμπνίγω.gloss: to strangle completely, i.e. (literally) to drown, or (figuratively) to crowd

συμπολίτης.gloss: a native of the same town, i.e. (figuratively) co-religionist (fellow-Christian)

συμπορεύομαι.gloss: to journey together; by implication, to assemble

συμπόσιον.gloss: a drinking-party ("symposium"), i.e. (by extension) a room of guests

συμφέρω.gloss: to bear together (contribute), i.e. (literally) to collect, or (figuratively) to conduce; especially (neuter participle as a noun) advantage

σύμφημι.gloss: to say jointly, i.e. assent to

συμφυλέτης.gloss: a co-tribesman, i.e. native of the same country

σύμφυτος.gloss: grown along with (connate), i.e. (figuratively) closely united to

συμφωνέω.gloss: to be harmonious, i.e. (figuratively) to accord (be suitable, concur) or stipulate (by compact)

συμφωνία.gloss: unison of sound ("symphony"), i.e. a concert of instruments (harmonious note)

σύμφωνος.gloss: sounding together (alike), i.e. (figuratively) accordant (neuter as noun, agreement)

συνάγω.gloss: to lead together, i.e. collect or convene; specially, to entertain (hospitably)

συναθλέω.gloss: to wrestle in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to seek jointly

συναίρω.gloss: to make up together, i.e. (figuratively) to compute (an account)

συνανάκειμαι.gloss: to recline in company with (at a meal)

συναναμίγνυμι.gloss: to mix up together, i.e. (figurative) associate with

συναναπαύομαι.gloss: to recruit oneself in company with

συναντάω.gloss: to meet with; figuratively, to occur

συναντιλαμβάνομαι.gloss: to take hold of opposite together, i.e. co-operate (assist)

συναπάγω.gloss: to take off together, i.e. transport with (seduce, passively, yield)

συναποθνήσκω.gloss: to decease (literally) in company with, or (figuratively), similarly to

συναπόλλυμι.gloss: to destroy (middle voice or passively, be slain) in company with

συναποστέλλω.gloss: to despatch (on an errand) in company with

συναρμολογέω.gloss: to render close-jointed together, i.e. organize compactly

σύνδεσμος.gloss: a joint tie, i.e. ligament, (figuratively) uniting principle, control

συνδέω.gloss: to bind with, i.e. (passively) be a fellow-prisoner (figuratively)

σύνδουλος.gloss: a co-slave, i.e. servitor or ministrant of the same master (human or divine)

συνεκλεκτός.gloss: chosen in company with, i.e. co-elect (fellow Christian)

συνελαύνω.gloss: to drive together, i.e. (figuratively) exhort (to reconciliation)

συνεπιμαρτυρέω.gloss: to testify further jointly, i.e. unite in adding evidence

συνεργέω.gloss: to be a fellow-worker, i.e. co-operate

συνεσθίω.gloss: to take food in company with

συνετός.gloss: mentally put (or putting) together, i.e. sagacious

συνευδοκέω.gloss: to think well of in common, i.e. assent to, feel gratified with

συνευωχέω.gloss: to entertain sumptuously in company with, i.e. (middle voice or passive) to revel together

συνεφίστημι.gloss: to stand up together, i.e. to resist (or assault) jointly

συνέχω.gloss: to hold together, i.e. to compress (the ears, with a crowd or siege) or arrest (a prisoner); figuratively, to compel, perplex, afflict, preoccupy

συντάσσω.gloss: to arrange jointly, i.e. (figuratively) to direct

συντίθεμαι.gloss: to place jointly, i.e. (figuratively) to consent (bargain, stipulate), concur

συνυπουργέω.gloss: to be a co-auxiliary, i.e. assist

φράζω.gloss: to indicate (by word or act), i.e. (specially), to expound

φράσσω.gloss: to fence or inclose, i.e. (specially), to block up (figuratively, to silence)

φρεναπατάω.gloss: to be a mind-misleader, i.e. delude

φρήν.gloss: the midrif (as a partition of the body), i.e. (figuratively and by implication, of sympathy) the feelings (or sensitive nature; by extension (also in the plural) the mind or cognitive faculties)

φρονέω.gloss: to exercise the mind, i.e. entertain or have a sentiment or opinion; by implication, to be (mentally) disposed (more or less earnestly in a certain direction); intensively, to interest oneself in (with concern or obedience)

φρόνησις.gloss: mental action or activity, i.e. intellectual or moral insight

φρόνιμος.gloss: thoughtful, i.e. sagacious or discreet (implying a cautious character; while denotes practical skill or acumen; and indicates rather intelligence or mental acquirement); in a bad sense conceited (also in the comparative)

φροντίζω.gloss: to exercise thought, i.e. be anxious

φρουρέω.gloss: to be a watcher in advance, i.e. to mount guard as a sentinel (post spies at gates); figuratively, to hem in, protect

φρυάσσω.gloss: to snort (as a spirited horse), i.e. (figuratively) to make a tumult

φρύγανον.gloss: something desiccated, i.e. a dry twig

φυλακή.gloss: a guarding or (concretely, guard), the act, the person; figuratively, the place, the condition, or (specially), the time (as a division of day or night), literally or figuratively

φυλακτήριον.gloss: a guard-case, i.e. "phylactery" for wearing slips of Scripture texts

φυλάσσω.gloss: to watch, i.e. be on guard (literally of figuratively); by implication, to preserve, obey, avoid

φυλή.gloss: an offshoot, i.e. race or clan

φύσις.gloss: growth (by germination or expansion), i.e. (by implication) natural production (lineal descent); by extension, a genus or sort; figuratively, native disposition, constitution or usage

φυτεία.gloss: trans-planting, i.e. (concretely) a shrub or vegetable

φυτεύω.gloss: to set out in the earth, i.e. implant; figuratively, to instil doctrine

φύω.gloss: probably originally, to "puff" or blow, i.e. to swell up; but only used in the implied sense, to germinate or grow (sprout, produce), literally or figuratively

φωνέω.gloss: to emit a sound (animal, human or instrumental); by implication, to address in words or by name, also in imitation

φωνή.gloss: a tone (articulate, bestial or artificial); by implication, an address (for any purpose), saying or language

φῶς.gloss: luminousness (in the widest application, natural or artificial, abstract or concrete, literal or figurative)

φωστήρ.gloss: an illuminator, i.e. (concretely) a luminary, or (abstractly) brilliancy

φωτίζω.gloss: to shed rays, i.e. to shine or (transitively) to brighten up (literally or figuratively)

χαίρω.gloss: to be "cheer"ful, i.e. calmly happy or well-off; impersonally, especially as salutation (on meeting or parting), be well

χαλεπός.gloss: difficult, i.e. dangerous, or (by implication) furious

χαλιναγωγέω.gloss: to be a bit-leader, i.e. to curb (figuratively)

χαλινός.gloss: a curb or head-stall (as curbing the spirit)

χαλκολίβανον.gloss: burnished copper, an alloy of copper (or gold) and silver having a brilliant lustre

χαλκός.gloss: copper (the substance, or some implement or coin made of it)

Χαναάν.gloss: Chanaan (i.e. Kenaan), the early name of Palestine

Χανααναῖος.gloss: a Chanaanæan (i.e. Kenaanite), or native of gentile Palestine

χάραγμα.gloss: a scratch or etching, i.e. stamp (as a badge of servitude), or sculptured figure (statue)

χαρακτήρ.gloss: a graver (the tool or the person), i.e. (by implication) engraving (("character"), the figure stamped, i.e. an exact copy or (figuratively) representation)

χάραξ.gloss: a stake, i.e. (by implication) a palisade or rampart (military mound for circumvallation in a siege)

χαρίζομαι.gloss: to grant as a favor, i.e. gratuitously, in kindness, pardon or rescue

χάριν.gloss: through favor of, i.e. on account of

χάρις.gloss: graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)

χάρισμα.gloss: a (divine) gratuity, i.e. deliverance (from danger or passion); (specially), a (spiritual) endowment, i.e. (subjectively) religious qualification, or (objectively) miraculous faculty

χαριτόω.gloss: to grace, i.e. indue with special honor

χάρτης.gloss: a sheet ("chart") of writing-material (as to be scribbled over)

χεῖλος.gloss: a lip (as a pouring place); figuratively, a margin (of water)

χειμάζω.gloss: to storm, i.e. (passively) to labor under a gale

χειμών.gloss: akin to the base of 5490 through the idea of a channel), meaning a storm (as pouring rain); by implication, the rainy season, i.e. winter

χείρ.gloss: the hand (literally or figuratively (power); especially (by Hebraism) a means or instrument)

χειραγωγέω.gloss: to be a hand-leader, i.e. to guide (a blind person)

χειραγωγός.gloss: a hand-leader, i.e. personal conductor (of a blind person)

χειρόγραφον.gloss: something hand-written ("chirograph"), i.e. a manuscript (specially, a legal document or bond (figuratively))

χειροτονέω.gloss: to be a hand-reacher or voter (by raising the hand), i.e. (generally) to select or appoint

χείρων.gloss: from an obsolete equivalent (of uncertain derivation); more evil or aggravated (physically, mentally or morally)

χήρα.gloss: a widow (as lacking a husband), literally or figuratively

χιλίαρχος.gloss: the commander of a thousand soldiers ("chiliarch"; i.e. colonel

χλευάζω.gloss: to throw out the lip, i.e. jeer at

χοϊκός.gloss: dusty or dirty (soil-like), i.e. (by implication) terrene

χολάω.gloss: to be bilious, i.e. (by implication) irritable (enraged, "choleric")

χολή.gloss: "gall" or bile, i.e. (by analogy) poison or an anodyne (wormwood, poppy, etc.)

χόος.gloss: a heap (as poured out), i.e. rubbish; loose dirt

χορηγέω.gloss: to be a dance-leader, i.e. (generally) to furnish

χορτάζω.gloss: to fodder, i.e. (generally) to gorge (supply food in abundance)

χόρτος.gloss: a "court" or "garden", i.e. (by implication, of pasture) herbage or vegetation

χράομαι.gloss: to furnish what is needed; (give an oracle, "graze" (touch slightly), light upon, etc.), i.e. (by implication) to employ or (by extension) to act towards one in a given manner

χρεία.gloss: employment, i.e. an affair; also (by implication) occasion, demand, requirement or destitution

χρῄζω.gloss: to make (i.e. have) necessity, i.e. be in want of

χρῆμα.gloss: something useful or needed, i.e. wealth, price

χρηματίζω.gloss: to utter an oracle (compare the original sense of ), i.e. divinely intimate; by implication, (compare the secular sense of ) to constitute a firm for business, i.e. (generally) bear as a title

χρῆσις.gloss: employment, i.e. (specially), sexual intercourse (as an occupation of the body)

χρηστεύομαι.gloss: to show oneself useful, i.e. act benevolently

χρηστός.gloss: employed, i.e. (by implication) useful (in manner or morals)

χρηστότης.gloss: usefulness, i.e. morally, excellence (in character or demeanor)

χρῖσμα.gloss: an unguent or smearing, i.e. (figuratively) the special endowment ("chrism") of the Holy Spirit

Χριστιανός.gloss: a Christian, i.e. follower of Christ

Χριστός.gloss: anointed, i.e. the Messiah, an epithet of Jesus

χρίω.gloss: to smear or rub with oil, i.e. (by implication) to consecrate to an office or religious service

χρόνος.gloss: a space of time (in general, and thus properly distinguished from , which designates a fixed or special occasion; and from , which denotes a particular period) or interval; by extension, an individual opportunity; by implication, delay

χρονοτριβέω.gloss: to be a time-wearer, i.e. to procrastinate (linger)

χρυσίον.gloss: a golden article, i.e. gold plating, ornament, or coin

χρυσοδακτύλιος.gloss: gold-ringed, i.e. wearing a golden finger-ring or similar jewelry

χρυσός.gloss: gold; by extension, a golden article, as an ornament or coin

χρυσόω.gloss: to gild, i.e. bespangle with golden ornaments

χρώς.gloss: the body (properly, its surface or skin)

χώρα.gloss: room, i.e. a space of territory (more or less extensive; often including its inhabitants)

χωρέω.gloss: to be in (give) space, i.e. (intransitively) to pass, enter, or (transitively) to hold, admit (literally or figuratively)

χωρίζω.gloss: to place room between, i.e. part; reflexively, to go away

χωρίον.gloss: a spot or plot of ground

χωρίς.gloss: at a space, i.e. separately or apart from (often as preposition)

ψάλλω.gloss: to twitch or twang, i.e. to play on a stringed instrument (celebrate the divine worship with music and accompanying odes)

ψαλμός.gloss: a set piece of music, i.e. a sacred ode (accompanied with the voice, harp or other instrument; a "psalm"); collectively, the book of the Psalms

ψευδάδελφος.gloss: a spurious brother, i.e. pretended associate

ψευδαπόστολος.gloss: a spurious apostle, i.e. pretended pracher

ψευδοδιδάσκαλος.gloss: a spurious teacher, i.e. propagator of erroneous Christian doctrine

ψευδολόγος.gloss: mendacious, i.e. promulgating erroneous Christian doctrine

ψεύδομαι.gloss: to utter an untruth or attempt to deceive by falsehood

ψευδομάρτυρ.gloss: a spurious witness, i.e. bearer of untrue testimony

ψευδομαρτυρέω.gloss: to be an untrue testifier, i.e. offer falsehood in evidence

ψευδοπροφήτης.gloss: a spurious prophet, i.e. pretended foreteller or religious impostor

ψηλαφάω.gloss: to manipulate, i.e. verify by contact; figuratively, to search for

ψηφίζω.gloss: to use pebbles in enumeration, i.e. (generally) to compute

ψῆφος.gloss: a pebble (as worn smooth by handling), i.e. (by implication, of use as a counter or ballot) a verdict (of acquittal) or ticket (of admission); a vote

ψυχή.gloss: breath, i.e. (by implication) spirit, abstractly or concretely (the animal sentient principle only; thus distinguished on the one hand from , which is the rational and immortal soul; and on the other from , which is mere vitality, even of plants: these terms thus exactly correspond respectively to the Hebrew , and )

ψυχικός.gloss: sensitive, i.e. animate (in distinction on the one hand from , which is the higher or renovated nature; and on the other from , which is the lower or bestial nature)

ψύχω.gloss: to breathe (voluntarily but gently, thus differing on the one hand from , which denotes properly a forcible respiration; and on the other from the base of , which refers properly to an inanimate breeze), i.e. (by implication, of reduction of temperature by evaporation) to chill (figuratively)

ψωμίζω.gloss: to supply with bits, i.e. (generally) to nourish

ψωμίον.gloss: a crumb or morsel (as if rubbed off), i.e. a mouthful

ψώχω.gloss: to triturate, i.e. (by analogy) to rub out (kernels from husks with the fingers or hand)

ὦ.gloss: (may, might, can, could, would, should, must, etc.; also with and its compounds, as well as with other particles) be

ὧδε.gloss: in this same spot, i.e. here or hither

ᾠδή.gloss: a chant or "ode" (the general term for any words sung; while denotes especially a religious metrical composition, and still more specially, a Hebrew cantillation)

ὠδίν.gloss: a pang or throe, especially of childbirth

ὠδίνω.gloss: to experience the pains of parturition (literally or figuratively)

ὦμος.gloss: the shoulder (as that on which burdens are borne)

ὡραῖος.gloss: belonging to the right hour or season (timely), i.e. (by implication) flourishing (beauteous (figuratively))

ὡς.gloss: which how, i.e. in that manner (very variously used, as follows)

ὡσαύτως.gloss: as thus, i.e. in the same way

ὡσπερεί.gloss: just as if, i.e. as it were

ὥστε.gloss: so too, i.e. thus therefore (in various relations of consecution, as follow)

ὠτίον.gloss: an earlet, i.e. one of the ears, or perhaps the lobe of the ear

ὠφελέω.gloss: to be useful, i.e. to benefit
